title: leucanthemum atratum subsp. ceratophylloides
intro: Leucanthemum atratum subsp. ceratophylloides, also known as the Ox-eye daisy, is a flowering plant in the daisy family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and wounds, due to its potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Leucanthemum atratum subsp. ceratophylloides can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties.
